hints: Python has built-ins for this: min(grades) gives the smallest value, max(grades) gives the largest, sum(grades) gives the total.
The average is sum(grades) / len(grades). Wrap it in round(..., 2) to get exactly two decimal places.
To return multiple values as a tuple, separate them with commas: return (lowest, highest, average). The parentheses are optional but make it clear.
The caller can unpack the tuple like this: low, high, avg = grade_stats(grades). That's tuple unpacking — each variable gets one element.
